title: ampelocissus hardyi
intro: Ampelocissus hardyi, also known as the Indian vine or elephant creeper, is a species of plant in the grape family native to the tropical regions of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and act as an antimicrobial agent. The health benefits of Ampelocissus hardyi include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, reduce blood pressure, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by drying and grinding the roots, leaves, or fruits, which can then be used to make teas, infusions, or capsules.
